lesson.title: OOP & Modules
lesson.body: Object-oriented programming in Python uses classes to bundle data and behavior. The __init__ method initializes instance attributes and self refers to the instance itself. Inheritance lets a child class reuse and extend a parent class using the class Child(Parent) syntax. Python's standard library provides powerful modules like os, sys, json, and datetime; third-party packages are installed via pip and imported with import.

rooms.question: Complete the class definition so that Dog('Rex') stores the name correctly:
rooms.explanation: __init__ is the initializer method called automatically when an instance is created. It sets up instance attributes.
Cat overrides the speak() method from Animal. Python's method resolution order finds Cat.speak() first, so 'Meow' is returned.

rooms.question: Complete the import to read a JSON file using the standard library:
rooms.code:
import ___

with open('data.json') as f:
    data = json.load(f)
rooms.explanation: The json module is part of Python's standard library and provides json.load() to parse a JSON file into a Python dict.

rooms.question: Complete the code to get the current working directory using os:
rooms.explanation: os.getcwd() returns the current working directory as a string. It is the os equivalent of the shell command pwd.

rooms.question: Order these steps to correctly define a child class that calls the parent constructor:
rooms.explanation: First declare the class inheriting from Parent, then define __init__, then call super().__init__() to initialize the parent's attributes, then set the child-specific attribute.

boss.name: The Import Error
boss.taunt: No module named 'your_knowledge'. Install it with pip install study_harder!

questions.question: What does super().__init__() do inside a child class?
questions.explanation: super() returns a proxy that delegates method calls to the parent class. super().__init__() runs the parent's initializer to set up inherited attributes.

questions.question: Complete the code to parse a date string into a datetime object:
questions.code:
from datetime import datetime
dt = datetime.___("2024-01-15", "%Y-%m-%d")
print(dt.year)
questions.explanation: datetime.strptime() parses a string into a datetime object using a format code. strftime() does the reverse — formats a datetime into a string.

questions.question: What is the correct way to import only the sqrt function from the math module?
questions.explanation: from math import sqrt imports only the sqrt name into the current namespace. import math.sqrt is invalid Python syntax.
